name: Issue ID tokens in the authorization code flow
outcome: An authorization code flow requesting the openid scope returns a signed, verifiable ID token alongside the access token.
scope: Add the ID token builder and wire it into the auth code grant's token response, gated on OIDC being enabled and the openid scope being granted. Also auto-recognize the standard OIDC scopes.
acceptance_criteria: An authorization code flow with scope=openid returns a signed id_token alongside the access token
The id_token verifies against the JWKS key and carries correct iss, sub, aud, exp, iat, conditional nonce/auth_time, and at_hash
sub is stringified and aud is the client id
A flow without the openid scope returns no id_token, and non-OIDC flows are unchanged
steps: src/oidc — add an ID token builder assembling protocol claims (iss, stringified sub, aud set to the client, exp from the access-token TTL, iat, conditional nonce and auth_time) and at_hash
AbstractGrant — auto-recognize the standard OIDC scopes (openid, profile, email, address, phone, offline_access) as valid when OIDC is enabled
AuthCodeGrant.respondToAccessTokenRequest — add a signed id_token to the token response body when OIDC is enabled and the openid scope was granted
index.ts — export the ID token claim types
test/unit and test/e2e — cover claim assembly, at_hash, and the openid-scope gate
notes: ID token aud is the client, deliberately different from the access token's resource aud. exp reuses the access-token TTL — no separate config knob. azp is omitted in v1. The ID token stays lean: protocol claims only.
